'''Doby Tyerell''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] from [[Aleen/Legends|Aleen]] who was the son of the famous [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Ratts Tyerell/Legends|Ratts Tyerell]] and the younger brother of [[Deland Tyerell/Legends|Deland Tyerell]].

==Biography==
When his father died in the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] podrace of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], his uncle sold Doby's older sister [[Djulla]] into slavery. Ratts' podracing colleague, [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], bought her. To get her freedom back, Doby's brother Deland would compete against Sebulba's son [[Hekula]] in an illegal podrace at the [[Great Dordon Caves]] on the outskirts of [[Eusebus|Euceron City]], held separately from but concurrent with the [[Galactic Games/Legends|Galactic Games]] of [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]]. Doby was Deland's race mechanic.

But, as always, Sebulba cheated, and he broke Deland's arm prior to the race. Fortunately, a young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] raced on Deland's behalf in his vehicle, as one of Sebulba's one-time rivals. Even though Deland's [[Podracer/Legends|podracer]] was sabotaged by [[Maxo Vista]], Anakin passed Hekula's pod<ref>Hekula piloted [[Anakin Skywalker's Podracer/Legends|Anakin's first podracer]] built on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which Sebulba had purchased from [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]].</ref> to win the race, freeing Djulla and sending the three happy [[Aleena/Legends|Aleenas]] back to their home.

[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] discovered, however, that Doby and Deland had cheated by obtaining advanced [[holograph]]ic route information (before it was revealed to the other racers) via a "timing" map fed to their pod by Vista. Even so, and despite what also proved to be Vista-enacted sabotage to their pod, Skywalker pulled through for them: the young Jedi's win secured the freedom of the Tyerell brothers' sister.
